data.content: This project uses MSTest with the Microsoft Testing Platform. The cleanest way to get crash dumps is to add the `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump` package and use the `--crashdump` flag.
data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run tests with `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host. 

This project uses MSTest with the Microsoft Testing Platform (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` is true). To collect crash dumps on test host crashes, the recommended approach is to use the `--crashdump` option (available through `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump` package) or environment variables like `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump`.

Since this project uses the Microsoft Testing Platform, the cleanest approach is to:

1. Add the `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump` NuGet package


2. Run `dotnet test --crashdump` to enable crash dump collection when the test host crashes.

index 0000000..0000000 100644

+++ b/tmp/sv-642826350c2a4f8993cabec0c929e7ed/TestProject.csproj

 </Project>

data.content: The `--crashdump` is a Testing Platform argument. Need to pass it through with `--`:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump via passthrough

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with crash dump via passthrough

data.reasoningText: The `--crashdump` flag isn't recognized as a `dotnet test` argument directly - it's a Microsoft Testing Platform argument. Since `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` is `true`, we need to pass it through using `--`. Let me try `dotnet test -- --crashdump`.

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump via passthrough

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-642826350c2a4f8993cabec0c929e7ed && dotnet test -- --crashdump 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with crash dump via passthrough", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's what I did:

1. **Added `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump`** (v1.6.2) to the project — this integrates with the Microsoft Testing Platform to automatically capture a crash dump if the test host process crashes.

2. **Run command**: `dotnet test -- --crashdump`

The `--` separator passes `--crashdump` through to the Testing Platform. If a test ever crashes the host, a `.dmp` file will be written to the test results directory. You can also use `--crashdump-type full` for a full memory dump instead of the default mini dump.
